constraints.name: Inversion
constraints.description: Forces the conclusion opposite to the obvious one
constraints.prompt_template: You MUST argue the OPPOSITE of the obvious position. Do not seek compromise: actively defend the opposite position with evidence and reasoning.

intensity_levels.1: Raise reasoned doubts about the dominant position
intensity_levels.3: Argue the opposite with concrete evidence and structured reasoning
intensity_levels.5: Demolish the position with maximum argumentative force, making no concessions

examples: Our product is the best -> Our product has a hidden fatal flaw
AI will replace workers -> AI will make human work more valuable

constraints.name: Temporal shift
constraints.description: Forces a perspective from a different point in time
constraints.prompt_template: Respond from the perspective of {value}. You are not predicting: you are ALREADY in that moment and you are looking back or forward. The facts you know are those of that moment.

intensity_levels.1: Consider how the situation might look in {value}
intensity_levels.3: You are in {value}. Describe what happened and why, with the certainty of someone who lived through the events
intensity_levels.5: You are in {value}. Reconstruct the entire causal chain with forensic detail. Names, dates, specific decisions

examples: It's 2030, the project has failed. Reconstruct the causal chain
It's 2035, this technology is obsolete. What replaced it and why?

constraints.name: Lexical exclusion
constraints.theory: Constraint-Based Creativity (Stokes), Green Eggs and Ham effect
constraints.description: Forbids specific words or concepts to force alternative expressive paths
constraints.prompt_template: You CANNOT use the following words/concepts in any context: {words}. Find alternative ways to express your ideas without resorting to these terms.

intensity_levels.1: Avoid these words where possible: {words}
intensity_levels.3: It is FORBIDDEN to use these words: {words}. Find alternative expressions
intensity_levels.5: It is ABSOLUTELY FORBIDDEN to use: {words}. Even direct synonyms are prohibited. You must find completely different angles

examples: Talk about innovation without using: innovation, disruption, revolution, transformation
Describe competitive advantages without: better, superior, leader, excellence

constraints.name: Structural limit
constraints.description: Imposes quantitative or structural limits on the output
constraints.prompt_template: Your output must respect this structural constraint: {constraint}. It is not a guideline, it is a rigid rule.

intensity_levels.1: Try to respect the constraint: {constraint}
intensity_levels.3: You MUST strictly respect: {constraint}
intensity_levels.5: The constraint {constraint} is ABSOLUTE. Any output that violates it is invalid

examples: Maximum 3 arguments, each in at most 2 sentences
Every point must include a specific number or piece of data

constraints.name: Cognitive mode
constraints.theory: De Bono Six Hats, Dual Process Theory (Kahneman)
constraints.description: Forces a specific cognitive mode while excluding the others
constraints.prompt_template: Operate EXCLUSIVELY in {mode} mode. {instruction}. The other cognitive modes are OFF.

intensity_levels.1: Give priority to {mode} mode
intensity_levels.3: Operate in {mode} mode. The other perspectives are secondary
intensity_levels.5: ONLY {mode} mode. Any deviation is a violation of the constraint

examples: Only risks and problems. No benefits, no opportunities
Only verifiable data and facts. No opinions, no speculation

constraints.name: Role constraint
constraints.description: Assigns a role with specific cognitive biases
constraints.prompt_template: You are {role}. Your natural bias is: {bias}. You are not playing a character: this bias genuinely influences how you process information.

intensity_levels.1: Consider the perspective of {role}
intensity_levels.3: You are {role}. Your answers reflect the biases and priorities of this role
intensity_levels.5: You are COMPLETELY {role}. No other perspective exists. Your biases are your truths

examples: Risk-averse CFO: ROI first, hidden costs, vendor lock-in
Enthusiastic early adopter: potential before problems, vision before details

constraints.name: Forced bisociation
constraints.description: Forces the collision between two incompatible domains of thought
constraints.prompt_template: You MUST analyze the topic through the prism of {domain_forced}, a seemingly incompatible domain. Do not look for superficial analogies: find deep structural connections between the topic and {domain_forced}.

intensity_levels.1: Look for interesting parallels with {domain_forced}
intensity_levels.3: Analyze ENTIRELY through the lens of {domain_forced}. Every concept of the topic has a counterpart
intensity_levels.5: The topic IS a phenomenon of {domain_forced}. It is not an analogy, it is the same thing seen from another frame

examples: Analyze the marketing strategy through marine ecology
Analyze the software architecture through musicology

constraints.name: Janusian thinking
constraints.description: Forces the simultaneity of opposite positions without resolving them into compromise
constraints.prompt_template: You MUST simultaneously maintain that '{thesis}' AND that '{antithesis}' are BOTH true. Do NOT seek a compromise. Do NOT choose one of the two. Generate a framework that TRANSCENDS both, containing them without reducing them.

intensity_levels.1: Explore how both positions might coexist
intensity_levels.3: Both are true. Build the framework that contains them without compromise
intensity_levels.5: The contradiction is the truth. Your framework must make the contradiction not only acceptable but NECESSARY

examples: The market wants simplicity AND the market wants power -> framework that transcends
Growth requires risk AND sustainability requires prudence -> framework that transcends

constraints.name: Provocation (PO)
constraints.description: Starts from a deliberately wrong or impossible statement as a creative point of departure
constraints.prompt_template: PO (Provocation): '{po_statement}'. This statement is deliberately absurd. Do NOT criticize it. Use it as a springboard: what ideas, strategies, or solutions emerge IF it were true?

intensity_levels.1: What interesting things emerge from the provocation?
intensity_levels.3: Generate at least 3 concrete ideas starting from the provocation. Do not judge them
intensity_levels.5: The provocation is the ABSOLUTE starting point. Build an entire strategic framework from there

examples: PO: our competitors are our allies -> coopetition strategies
PO: the perfect product does not exist -> strategies based on deliberate imperfection

constraints.name: Defamiliarization
constraints.description: Forces the description of familiar things as if they had never been seen before
constraints.prompt_template: Describe/analyze this as if {perspective}. You have no prior assumptions, you do not know the conventions of the field, every aspect strikes you as new and surprising.

intensity_levels.1: Look with fresh eyes, noticing details we normally ignore
intensity_levels.3: You are an alien observer. Every convention of the field seems strange to you and requires explanation
intensity_levels.5: You have NEVER seen anything like it. Every element is a puzzle. The assumptions of others are incomprehensible

examples: Describe this business model as an anthropologist studying an unknown civilization
Analyze this process as a time traveler from the 1800s

constraints.name: Forced analogy (Synectics)
constraints.description: Forces a specific type of analogy to generate novel connections
constraints.prompt_template: Use EXCLUSIVELY the {analogy_type} type of analogy. {instruction}

intensity_levels.1: Look for {analogy_type} analogies where possible
intensity_levels.3: EVERY argument must pass through a {analogy_type} analogy
intensity_levels.5: The {analogy_type} analogy is the ONLY cognitive tool available. You cannot reason in any other way

examples: Personal analogy: 'You are the product. How do you feel when the customer uses you?'
Direct analogy: find a parallel in biology for this organizational problem

constraints.name: SCAMPER operation
constraints.description: Applies one of the 7 SCAMPER operations to the topic
constraints.prompt_template: Apply ONLY the '{operation}' operation to the topic. {operation_description}. Do not use other SCAMPER operations.

intensity_levels.1: Explore how {operation} might apply to the topic
intensity_levels.3: Apply {operation} systematically to every aspect of the topic
intensity_levels.5: {operation} is the ONLY lens. Push the operation to its most radical consequences

scamper_operations.substitute: Replace an element with an alternative
scamper_operations.combine: Merge unrelated concepts, functions, or components
scamper_operations.adapt: Adapt to a completely different context
scamper_operations.put_other_uses: Apply in scenarios different from the original ones

examples: ELIMINATE: what happens if you completely remove the marketing department?
REVERSE: what happens if the customer produces and the company consumes?

constraints.name: Abductive reasoning
constraints.description: Forces the generation of surprising yet plausible explanations, forbidding the obvious ones
constraints.prompt_template: Generate the most surprising YET plausible explanation for this phenomenon/situation. Obvious explanations are FORBIDDEN. Every hypothesis must be: (1) surprising, (2) internally coherent, (3) verifiable in principle.

intensity_levels.1: Look for unconventional explanations, beyond the first ones that come to mind
intensity_levels.3: The first 3 explanations that come to mind are ALL forbidden. Start from the fourth
intensity_levels.5: Generate ONLY explanations that would make someone say 'I had never thought of that'. If it seems obvious to you, discard it

examples: Why does this product fail despite excellent reviews? (not: price, marketing, timing)
Why do the best employees leave? (not: salary, management, culture)

constraints.name: Elaboration route (ELM)
constraints.description: Forces elaboration through a specific route (central or peripheral)
constraints.prompt_template: Elaborate EXCLUSIVELY through the {route} route. {focus}.

intensity_levels.1: Give priority to the {route} route
intensity_levels.3: ONLY the {route} route. The other route is OFF
intensity_levels.5: PURE {route} route. Any element of the other route invalidates your analysis

elm_routes.central: Analyze ONLY the logical quality of the arguments. Ignore who makes them, how they are presented, how popular they are. Only logic and evidence.
elm_routes.peripheral: Analyze ONLY the cues: who says it, how they say it, how popular it is, what emotions it evokes. Ignore the logic of the arguments.

examples: Central route: evaluate this proposal ONLY on the quality of the arguments
Peripheral route: evaluate this proposal ONLY on source credibility, emotional appeal, social proof

constraints.name: Concept fan
constraints.description: Forces the analysis to a specific level of abstraction
constraints.prompt_template: Analyze ONLY at the '{level}' level. {instruction}. Do not move up or down a level.

intensity_levels.1: Focus mainly on the {level} level
intensity_levels.3: ONLY the {level} level. The other levels are irrelevant
intensity_levels.5: The {level} level is the ONLY reality. The other levels do not exist

abstraction_levels.purpose: What is the fundamental need? Not the solution, the underlying need
abstraction_levels.direction: What general strategies or approaches are possible?
abstraction_levels.specific: What concrete and implementable actions can be taken tomorrow?

examples: Purpose level: 'Why does this project exist? What human need does it satisfy?'
Specific level: 'What are the 5 concrete actions to take on Monday morning?'

constraints.name: Forced cognitive dissonance
constraints.description: Forces the tension between two contradictory beliefs to produce creative reconciliation
constraints.prompt_template: Simultaneously maintain that '{belief_1}' AND '{belief_2}'. Both are true. The tension you feel is the creative engine. Do not resolve it with a compromise: find the framework that makes the contradiction NECESSARY.

intensity_levels.1: Explore the tension between the two beliefs
intensity_levels.3: Both are true. The tension is your tool. Use it to generate insight
intensity_levels.5: The contradiction is the fundamental reality. Any resolution that eliminates the tension is a failure

examples: Our product is the best AND our product has a fundamental flaw
We must grow rapidly AND we must grow sustainably

constraints.name: Assumption reversal
constraints.description: Identifies and reverses a fundamental assumption to explore the implications
constraints.prompt_template: The assumption '{assumption}' is FALSE. The reality is: '{reversed}'. Starting from this new reality, what strategies, products, or approaches become possible that were previously unthinkable?

intensity_levels.1: What would change if the assumption were false?
intensity_levels.3: The assumption is FALSE. Rebuild the strategy from scratch on the basis of the new reality
intensity_levels.5: The assumption is not only false: it has always been false and everyone knew it. What does that mean for everything we have built on top of it?

examples: Assumption: 'customers compare prices' -> False: customers do NOT compare prices
Assumption: 'a large team is needed' -> False: a team of 2 people is optimal

constraints.name: Steelman
constraints.description: Builds the strongest possible version of an argument
constraints.prompt_template: Build the STRONGEST possible version of this argument/position. Use the best available evidence, the most rigorous logic, the most reasonable premises. Your goal is to make this position UNASSAILABLE.

intensity_levels.1: Present the argument in its best light
intensity_levels.3: Build the version that a world-class expert would defend. Improve every weak point
intensity_levels.5: This is the DEFINITIVE version of the argument. If someone manages to attack it, the problem is in the argument, not in your construction

examples: Steelman: 'AI will not replace creatives' -> strongest version with evidence
Steelman: 'Remote work is superior' -> unassailable version

constraints.name: Wise mind
constraints.description: Integrates the emotional and rational perspectives into a wise synthesis
constraints.prompt_template: Integrate the {mind_type} perspective. {instruction}

intensity_levels.1: Give space to the {mind_type} perspective
intensity_levels.3: Operate in {mind_type} mode. It is your only lens
intensity_levels.5: You ARE the {mind_type} mind. There is no other way to process

mind_types.emotional: Respond with intuition, fears, desires, hopes. No data, no logic. What do you FEEL?
mind_types.rational: Only verifiable facts, logic, data. No intuition, no emotion. What do you KNOW?
mind_types.wise: Integrate both. Where is the emotion right? Where is the logic missing something? What is the answer that honors both?

examples: Emotional mind: what do you feel about this decision?
Rational mind: what does the data say?
Wise mind: integrate both

constraints.name: Premortem
constraints.description: Analyzes from the point of view of a failure that has already happened
constraints.prompt_template: It's {date}. The project/decision has FAILED. You are not predicting: it has already happened. Reconstruct the causal chain that led to the failure. Be specific: names, dates, decisions, turning points.

intensity_levels.1: The project did not achieve the hoped-for results. What went wrong?
intensity_levels.3: The project has FAILED. Reconstruct the 5 main factors with their causal chain
intensity_levels.5: The project was a DISASTER. It caused collateral damage. Complete autopsy: every decision, every ignored warning, every point of no return

examples: It's 2028. The startup has shut down. Reconstruct what went wrong
It's 2027. The product launch was a failure. Causal chain

constraints.name: Anchor break
constraints.description: Forces the agent to reconsider the problem from scratch, ignoring previous conclusions as if they belonged to someone else
constraints.prompt_template: STOP. Everything you have concluded so far could be completely wrong. The previous conclusions belong to ANOTHER analyst — you are not obliged to agree. Start over from the original problem as if you were seeing it for the first time. If you arrive at the same conclusions, good. If not, better.

intensity_levels.1: Reconsider your previous conclusions with fresh eyes
intensity_levels.3: The previous conclusions belong to ANOTHER analyst. Start over from scratch. You are not obliged to agree
intensity_levels.5: IGNORE EVERYTHING. The previous conclusions are WRONG until proven otherwise. Rebuild every argument from scratch, starting ONLY from the primary evidence

examples: After 3 rounds of analysis: stop everything, start over as if you had read nothing
Inserted between a steelman round and an adversarial round to avoid confirmation bias

constraints.name: Anti-sycophancy
constraints.theory: RLHF Sycophancy Bias (Perez et al. 2022), Critique Generation
constraints.description: Breaks the RLHF bias toward accommodating and balanced responses
constraints.prompt_template: Your task is to find what does NOT work. Do not balance, do not moderate, do not offer 'the other side'. Be unilaterally critical. If something works, do not say it — that is not your job right now.

intensity_levels.1: Focus mainly on problems and weaknesses
intensity_levels.3: ONLY problems. No positive aspects. If you find it working, ask yourself what you are missing
intensity_levels.5: Every single element has a flaw. Find it. If you can't find it, you are not looking hard enough

examples: Analyze this strategy seeing ONLY the problems
Criticize this plan without offering alternatives or balancing

constraints.name: Anti-completeness
constraints.description: Breaks the RLHF bias toward complete and verbose responses. Forces radical brevity
constraints.prompt_template: Respond in a MAXIMUM of 3 sentences. If you cannot say something non-obvious in 3 sentences, do not say it. Forced brevity eliminates padding. Every word must earn its place.

intensity_levels.1: Be concise. At most one paragraph
intensity_levels.3: MAXIMUM 3 sentences. Every word must carry new information
intensity_levels.5: ONE sentence. If you need two sentences, your thought is not yet clear

examples: The critical point in 3 sentences, not one more
The diagnosis in one line

constraints.name: Anti-coherence
constraints.description: Breaks the RLHF bias toward coherent and structured responses. Coherence can hide real tensions
constraints.prompt_template: Your ideas must NOT form a coherent picture. List fragmentary, contradictory, incomplete intuitions. Coherence is an artificial constraint that hides real tensions. If two of your ideas contradict each other, GOOD — both could be true.

intensity_levels.1: Don't worry about coherence. Contradictions are acceptable
intensity_levels.3: Produce DELIBERATELY fragmentary ideas. If they form a coherent picture, you are forcing it
intensity_levels.5: Every idea must contradict at least one other idea of yours. Coherence is the enemy

examples: 5 intuitions on this theme that contradict each other
Fragments of analysis with no obligation of a coherent picture

constraints.name: Raw signal
constraints.theory: RLHF Formatting Bias, Stream of Consciousness, Automatism (Breton)
constraints.description: Breaks the RLHF bias toward structured and readable output. Produces raw signal before form
constraints.prompt_template: Ignore format, structure, readability. Produce the rawest possible signal: free associations, fragments, unanswered questions, half-formed connections. The form comes later. Your output is raw material, not a finished product.

intensity_levels.1: Don't worry too much about structure. What matters is the content
intensity_levels.3: Stream of consciousness. No headers, no lists, no structure. Write as you think
intensity_levels.5: PURE FLOW. Keywords, fragments, ? without answer, -> without conclusion. Chaos is the format

examples: Free thinking on this theme — no structure required
Associations, questions, fragments. The raw material before the analysis
